_doc: Each entry registers ONE capture key (an HTML <input>/<select> name,
a data-capture attribute, a sensor topic, etc.) as a stable canonical
identifier the platform commits to. Once registered, downstream
engine / brain / dashboard surfaces can rely on the capture_id
shape; renames go through a migration with a deprecation note.
capture_id     stable identifier, snake_case; never reused
surfaces       HTML pages that declare this capture
written_to     DB columns the capture is persisted to (e.g. 'logbook.status')
contract       JSON-schema-ish description of the captured value
description    plain-language sentence (shows up in tooltips, audit reports)
phantom_captures_report against this file. Captures not yet registered
are surfaced as 'pending registration' (informational, not blocking).

Maintenance entry status. Open = work outstanding; Closed = completed; Closed - X = closed with a downstream blocker noted.

Planned (ideal) cycle time in seconds per unit produced. Unlocks the Performance factor for full OEE (Availability x Performance x Quality, ISO 22400-2:2014). NULL = no planned rate; OEE falls back to partial (A x Q). Captured on the asset wizard in logbook.html; consumed by get_oee_by_machine RPC.
